stories.headline: Trump reportedly berates Netanyahu over Israel’s Lebanon military actions

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: "You're f****** crazy. You'd be in prison if it weren't for me. I'm saving your a***. Everybody hates you now. Everybody hates Israel because of this." That's what a U.S. official tells Axios President Trump unloaded on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during a heated phone call over Israel's military actions in Lebanon.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: JUST IN: 🇺🇸🇮🇱 Axios sources say President Trump was "pissed" during call with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu and told him off: "You're f*cking crazy. You'd be in prison if it weren't for me. I'm saving your ass. Everybody hates you now. Everybody hates Israel because of this." "What the f*ck are you doing?"

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: So, Netanyahu is now openly boasting to the world he doesn’t care what the President of the United States thinks or says. Over to you, Mr President @realDonaldTrump - who’s the boss?

stories.headline: Massive Russian missile and drone strike hits Kyiv, killing dozens

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: A Chinese-owned Zeekr electric car dealership burns in the Ukrainian capital of Kyiv, after being targeted and destroyed during last night’s cruise and ballistic missile attack by Russia.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Today has been a difficult day in Ukraine after a massive Russian strike. There were many ballistic missiles and drones. ... Just today, Russian strikes have taken 22 lives in Kyiv and Dnipro, including children.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: Russia has just launched a massive air raid on Ukraine as intelligence reports have predicted for the last couple days.

stories.headline: Trump tells Iran 'make a Deal' as negotiations reportedly collapse amid war concerns

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: "As I told Iran, 'It’s time, one way or another, for you to make a Deal. You’ve been doing this for 47 years, and it cannot be allowed to go on any longer!'" - President Donald J. Trump 🇺🇸

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: Asked today how he feels about negotiations with Iran collapsing, Trump said, "I couldn't care less." You'd think the president of the United States would care about service members dying and millions paying more for gas because of his illegal and unconstitutional war.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: The more Netanyahu prevents the war with Iran from ending, the more obvious it becomes that he convinced Trump to start it.

stories.headline: Theo Von discusses COVID immunity with Dr. McCullough
stories.body:
THEO VON: “Was there anybody who was immune to COVID-19?”

DR. MCCULLOUGH: “There’s one adult group. You’re going to laugh.”

[Theo Von listens closely for the reveal]

DR. MCCULLOUGH: “Smokers… They got very mild cases. And they don’t get long COVID.”

THEO VON: “Why?”

MCCULLOUGH: “Because smokers maintain a level of nicotine in the bloodstream… Smoking blocks the spike protein. It’s amazing. I thought smokers were going to go down.”

THEO VON: “Do you think that’s a good idea [to use nicotine patches] on a regular basis?”

DR. MCCULLOUGH: “I think [it’s a good idea] if they have long COVID... Nicotine, don’t forget, is a nootropic. A nootropic is a drug that makes the brain function more effectively... It’s addictive, but it’s not harmful to the human body... Nicotine patches are perfectly safe.”

stories.headline: Lex Fridman shares experience with Jensen Huang in Taiwan
stories.body:
I got to spend all day today with Jensen in Taiwan: talking with thousands of engineers and eating street food at a night market. Jensen is received as a rockstar in Taiwan, like it's Beatles in the 60's. It's mind-blowing and fun to watch. But most importantly, through all the interactions and all my conversations with him, he remained the same humble, kind, thoughtful, funny guy he always was, even as a kid who went to these same night markets many years ago.

Btw, we tried a crazy amount of different street food. It's legit some of the most delicious food I've ever had. I can't wait to share video of it, including a ton of our conversations and hangout. When I can pause for a moment from all the travel to edit the video, I'll post it.

Can't wait to continue talking to Jensen and engineers at Computex this week, and exploring more of Taiwan, and of course roaming the night markets for some more delicious street food.

Days like these, even more than usual, I feel like the luckiest kid in the world.

Love you all! ❤️

stories.headline: Robert Pattinson asked to read script for Nolan's The Odyssey unlike other actors.
stories.body:
Every actor instantly said yes to Christopher Nolan’s ‘THE ODYSSEY’ except than Robert Pattinson who asked to read the script.

“You want to read it? Everyone else just said yes,’ Nolan replied.

(Source: https://www.gq.com/story/matt-damon-robert-pattinson-tom-holland-cover-interview-summer-2026

stories.headline: Robert Pattinson was only actor to request script for Nolan's The Odyssey.
stories.body:
Robert Pattinson was the only actor to ask to read the script for Christopher Nolan’s ‘THE ODYSSEY’ before agreeing to star in the film.

“You want to read it? Everyone else just said yes,” Nolan replied.

stories.headline: Bernie Sanders proposes taxing AI companies' stock 50%
stories.body:
Taxing 50% of AI companies' stock to ensure that AI benefits humanity isn't radical, but let me tell you what is:

Since Trump was elected, the 7 richest men in America, all Big Tech oligarchs, became $1.15 trillion richer while 60% live paycheck to paycheck.

That's radical.

stories.headline: NIH researchers arrested for conspiring to smuggle monkeypox into US, confirming conspiracy theories
stories.body:
🚨SHOCKING: TWO RESEARCHERS OF THE NIH HAVE JUST BEEN ARRESTED FOR CONSPIRING TO BRING MONKEYPOX INTO THE U.S.🚨

The conspiracy theories have been confirmed as REAL CONSPIRACIES.

For years, Americans were told that every outbreak, every pandemic scare, every emerging virus was simply an unfortunate act of nature.

Trust the experts.

Trust the institutions.

Trust the scientists.

Now we learn that TWO NIH researchers have reportedly been charged with conspiring to smuggle monkeypox into the United States.

Read that again.

Not random people.

Not foreign spies.

Researchers connected to one of the most powerful medical institutions on Earth.

And we’re supposed to believe this is just another isolated incident?

How many times have we heard the phrase “nothing to see here” before discovering there was, in fact, something to see?

The public is told there was no danger because the samples were reportedly inactivated.

Maybe that’s true.

Maybe it isn’t.

But that’s not the question Americans should be asking.

The question is:

Why allegedly smuggle anything at all?

Why bypass procedures?

Why risk federal charges if everything was legitimate?

And perhaps most importantly…

How many similar incidents never become public?

Most people assume every biological sample moving across international borders is tracked, documented, and heavily monitored.

But if federal prosecutors are willing to bring charges against government researchers, what does that say about the oversight system itself?

The same institutions that constantly demand trust are once again asking the public not to ask too many questions.

Sorry.

That’s not how trust works.

Trust is earned through transparency.

And every time another story like this surfaces, millions of Americans start wondering whether they’re seeing the tip of the iceberg… or just another crack in a dam that’s already beginning to fail.

👀

What do you think?

Coincidence?

Rogue individuals?

Or evidence that the public knows far less about what happens behind laboratory doors than we’ve been led to believe?

#Monkeypox #NIH #FBI #BreakingNews #Transparency #QuestionEverything

stories.headline: China's Nostradamus warns Trump's UFO declassifications could lead to societal atrocities
stories.body:
🚨🇨🇳 CHINA'S NOSTRADAMUS DROPS BOMBSHELL ON TRUMP'S UFO FILES: "ATROCITIES ARE COMING" 👽💥

As the Trump administration ramps up its historic UFO/UAP declassifications under the PURSUE program, China's Nostradamus, Professor Jiang Xueqin is sounding the alarm.

Jiang, the Chinese-Canadian futurist who gained fame for correctly predicting Trump's 2024 win and escalating US-Iran tensions, slammed the releases in a recent interview with YouTuber Sneako. 

He called alien/UFO claims "complete nonsense" and "BS", no extraterrestrials, no reverse engineered tech, just "hallucination" and distraction.

Instead of little green men, Jiang warns the real danger is societal collapse. People retreating into polarized "bubbles", UFO believers vs. skeptics, AI doomers, conspiracy theorists, fueled by fear and distrust. 

This echo-chamber effect, he says, could spark "atrocities" in the future, mirroring how empires crumble through internal division and civil unrest.

Trump's releases include videos of glowing eight pointed star like objects, metallic orbs, and high speed anomalies over military sites. While enthusiasts link them to non human intelligence (NHI), Jiang sees it as elite misdirection, keeping the public chasing shadows while deeper fractures widen.

With  billions of views on the PURSUE portal and ongoing tranches promised, this isn't fringe, it's mainstream fuel for division at a time of global tensions (Iran conflict, tech arms races). 

Is disclosure a psyop to erode trust? Or the gateway to paradigm-shifting NHI revelations? Jiang bets on the former leading to chaos.

History shows belief systems can divide nations. As more files drop, will we unite in curiosity... or fracture further? The atrocities he predicts may not come from the stars, but from us.

What do you think, distraction or disclosure?
